make.name: Make
make.short_description: Make is a visual no-code platform for building, automating, and integrating workflows that connect apps, data, and AI logic.
make.starting_price: Free (paid plans from around $9/month billed annually)

features.description: Build automations as a flowchart on a drag-and-drop canvas, making complex, branching logic easy to follow and edit.
features.title: Visual scenario builder

features.description: Connect popular CRMs, spreadsheets, email tools, e-commerce platforms, and more through pre-built modules.
features.title: Thousands of app integrations

features.description: Use generic HTTP requests, webhooks, and custom API calls to integrate services that don't have a native connector.
features.title: Custom API and webhook modules

features.description: Split a single scenario into multiple conditional paths so different data can trigger different downstream actions.
features.title: Routers and branching logic

features.description: Iterators, aggregators, and built-in functions reshape, filter, and combine data as it flows between apps.
features.title: Data mapping and transformation tools

features.description: Attach error handlers, retry logic, and fallback routes to scenarios so failures don't silently break a workflow.
features.title: Error handling and retries

features.description: Start from a library of pre-built templates for common automation patterns instead of building from scratch.
features.title: Scenario templates

features.description: Inspect past runs and data bundles at each step to debug issues and understand exactly what happened in a scenario.
features.title: Execution logs and history

features.description: Incorporate large language model steps and AI agent building blocks directly into automation workflows.
features.title: AI modules and agents

features.description: Run scenarios on a schedule or trigger them instantly via webhooks for near real-time automation.
features.title: Scheduling and instant triggers

pros: Visual, flowchart-style builder makes complex multi-branch automations easier to understand than list-based tools
Very wide reach through native integrations plus generic HTTP and webhook modules for almost any API
Strong data mapping and transformation tools for reshaping information between apps
Free plan and template library make it accessible to try before committing to a paid plan
Detailed execution logs and error-handling options support serious debugging and reliability
cons: Steeper learning curve than simpler, wizard-driven automation tools for first-time users
Usage-based operations/credits pricing can make costs less predictable for high-volume workflows
Some advanced concepts (bundles, iterators, aggregators) require a bit of trial and error to master
Very deep, vendor-certified integrations for specific enterprise systems may be thinner than dedicated point solutions
Complex scenarios with many modules can become visually cluttered and harder to navigate on the canvas

faqs.answer: Yes, Make offers a free plan with a limited monthly allowance of operations/credits, which is enough for light automation use. Paid plans remove those limits and add features like higher execution priority and more credits.
faqs.question: Is Make free to use?

faqs.answer: Make was previously known as Integromat. It was founded in 2012, acquired by Celonis in 2020, and rebranded as Make in 2022 as part of a broader repositioning of the product.
faqs.question: What was Make called before?

faqs.answer: No, Make is designed as a no-code visual builder, so most automations can be created by dragging and connecting modules. That said, comfort with concepts like JSON and APIs helps when working with custom HTTP requests or advanced data mapping.
faqs.question: Do I need to know how to code to use Make?

faqs.answer: Both are automation platforms, but Make emphasizes a visual, flowchart-style canvas that shows branching logic and data flow directly, while Zapier traditionally favors a simpler, more linear step-by-step setup.
faqs.question: How is Make different from Zapier?

faqs.answer: Yes, Make is built for scenarios with routers, loops, error handlers, and multiple branching paths, which makes it well suited to more elaborate workflows beyond simple one-to-one app connections.
faqs.question: Can Make handle complex, multi-step business processes?

features: Access to the visual scenario builder
Access to thousands of app integrations
Multi-user access with team roles and permissions
Shared scenario templates across a team
pricing_plans.price: From around $29/month (Teams); custom pricing for Enterprise

microsoft-power-bi.name: Microsoft Power BI
microsoft-power-bi.short_description: Microsoft's BI and data visualization platform for building interactive reports and dashboards from virtually any data source.

features.description: Connect to and transform data from hundreds of sources, including databases, cloud services, and spreadsheets, before it enters your model.
features.title: Power Query

features.description: Build relationships between tables and write custom calculations and measures using DAX to create a reusable semantic model.
features.title: DAX & Data Modeling

features.description: Drag-and-drop report design with drill-through, bookmarks, and filtering for exploring data visually.
features.title: Interactive Reports & Dashboards

features.description: Ask questions about your data in plain English and get an automatically generated chart or answer.
features.title: Natural Language Q&A

features.description: Built-in forecasting, clustering, and key influencer visuals, plus Copilot-assisted report creation and narrative summaries.
features.title: AI Visuals & Copilot

features.description: Control what each user can see within a shared dataset, and certify or endorse trusted reports across the organization.
features.title: Row-Level Security & Governance

features.description: Keep cloud reports current with scheduled and incremental refresh, including secure connections to on-premises data via gateways.
features.title: Scheduled Refresh & Gateways

features.description: Create pixel-perfect, printable operational reports for invoices, statements, and other fixed-layout documents.
features.title: Paginated Reports

features.description: View and interact with dashboards on iOS, Android, and Windows devices while away from a desktop.
features.title: Mobile Apps

features.description: Embed reports in Teams, SharePoint, and Power Apps, and trigger workflows with Power Automate.
features.title: Power Platform Integration

pros: Free Power BI Desktop is fully featured for building and modeling reports, so individuals can start without paying upfront
Deep integration with Excel, Teams, SharePoint, and Azure lowers friction for organizations already on Microsoft 365
DAX and Power Query give advanced users precise control over calculations and data transformation beyond drag-and-drop charting
Enterprise-grade governance, including row-level security, certified datasets, and deployment pipelines, supports large, IT-managed analytics programs
Frequent monthly feature updates from Microsoft keep the platform evolving quickly, including new AI-assisted capabilities
cons: Licensing can get complex once you factor in Pro, Premium Per User, and capacity-based Premium tiers
DAX and proper data modeling have a real learning curve for users expecting simple spreadsheet-style charting
Power BI Desktop is Windows-only, so Mac users need a browser or a virtual machine to author reports
Sharing and collaboration features require paid licensing, such as Pro or Premium, even for small teams
Large or complex data models can hit performance limits without capacity-level Premium licensing or careful optimization

faqs.answer: Power BI Desktop, the report-authoring app, is free to download and use. Sharing reports with others, scheduling refreshes, and collaborating in the Power BI service generally require a paid Pro or Premium license per user.
faqs.question: Is Power BI free?

faqs.answer: Pro is a per-user license enabling sharing and collaboration. Premium Per User adds larger dataset limits, paginated reports, and more advanced AI features per user. Premium capacity is an organization-wide license providing dedicated compute for larger workloads, priced per capacity rather than per seat.
faqs.question: What's the difference between Power BI Pro and Premium?

faqs.answer: Power BI Desktop is a Windows-only application. Mac users typically access reports through the Power BI service in a web browser or run Desktop via a Windows virtual machine.
faqs.question: Does Power BI work on Mac?

faqs.answer: Yes, Power BI includes connectors for hundreds of data sources, including SQL Server, Excel, SharePoint, Salesforce, Google Analytics, and various Azure services, plus a data gateway for secure on-premises refresh.
faqs.question: Can Power BI connect to my existing databases and cloud apps?

faqs.answer: Basic report building and drag-and-drop visualizations don't require code, but getting the most out of Power BI, including custom calculations and advanced data modeling, benefits from learning DAX and the Power Query M language.
faqs.question: Do I need to know how to code to use Power BI?

features: Full report authoring and data modeling

microsoft-teams.name: Microsoft Teams
microsoft-teams.short_description: Chat, video meetings, and file collaboration built into Microsoft 365, connecting teams across Office apps in one workspace.

features.description: Organize conversations into teams and channels with threaded replies, reactions, and searchable history.
features.title: Persistent Chat & Channels

features.description: Host video calls with screen sharing, recording, live captions, and background effects.
features.title: HD Video Meetings

features.description: Edit Word, Excel, and PowerPoint files together directly inside chats and channels.
features.title: Real-Time Office Co-Authoring

features.description: Split larger meetings into smaller discussion groups and bring participants back together.
features.title: Breakout Rooms

features.description: Make and receive VoIP calls, transfer calls, and manage voicemail from within Teams.
features.title: Teams Phone

features.description: Connect third-party apps, bots, and workflows as tabs or notifications within a channel.
features.title: App & Workflow Integrations

features.description: Record meetings and generate transcripts for participants to review afterward.
features.title: Meeting Recording & Transcription

features.description: Use immersive meeting views designed to reduce video-call fatigue in large groups.
features.title: Together Mode & Custom Layouts

features.description: Manage security policies, data retention, and user access from a centralized admin console.
features.title: Admin Controls & Compliance

pros: Deep, native integration with Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and SharePoint
Free tier available alongside plans bundled into Microsoft 365 subscriptions
Combines chat, video meetings, calling, and file collaboration in one app
Strong admin, security, and compliance controls for larger organizations
Works across desktop, web, and mobile with consistent functionality
cons: Interface can feel dense and overwhelming for teams new to Microsoft 365
Notification management requires deliberate setup to avoid channel overload
Less compelling for organizations not already using Microsoft 365 or Office
Full feature set often requires a paid Microsoft 365 license tier
Some users find channel and team structure less intuitive than simpler chat apps

faqs.answer: Yes, Microsoft Teams offers a free plan with chat, video meetings, and basic file sharing. Additional features and higher usage limits require a paid Microsoft 365 or Teams Essentials plan.
faqs.question: Is Microsoft Teams free to use?

faqs.answer: No, Teams can be used with the free plan or standalone Teams Essentials without a full Microsoft 365 subscription, though bundled Microsoft 365 plans unlock deeper Office integration.
faqs.question: Do I need Microsoft 365 to use Teams?

faqs.answer: Yes, with a Teams Phone add-on or included plan, users can make and receive calls, transfer calls, and manage voicemail, functioning as a cloud-based phone system.
faqs.question: Can Microsoft Teams replace a phone system?

faqs.answer: Yes, Microsoft Teams has apps for iOS and Android that support chat, meetings, calling, and file access alongside the desktop and web clients.
faqs.question: Does Teams work on mobile devices?

faqs.answer: Teams combines chat similar to Slack and video meetings similar to Zoom into one app, with its main advantage being tighter native integration with Microsoft 365 and Office applications.
faqs.question: How does Teams compare to Slack or Zoom?

features: Video meetings for up to 60 minutes
Longer group meetings and larger meeting sizes
Standalone plan without full Microsoft 365 apps
Teams plus web and mobile versions of Office apps
Full desktop versions of Word, Excel, and PowerPoint

wetransfer.name: WeTransfer
wetransfer.short_description: WeTransfer lets you send large files and share photos, videos, and documents online in seconds, no account needed for recipients.

features.description: Send files without an email attachment limit; recipients download directly from a link without needing a WeTransfer account.
features.title: Large File Transfers

features.description: Add a password to a transfer so only people with the code can open and download the files.
features.title: Password Protection

features.description: Control how long a download link stays active, from a short default window on free accounts to longer custom periods on paid plans.
features.title: Custom Link Expiration

features.description: See when a transfer has been opened or downloaded so you know a delivery has been received.
features.title: Transfer Tracking

features.description: Apply your own branding to the page recipients see when downloading files, available on paid tiers.
features.title: Branded Transfer Pages

features.description: Generate a link that lets other people upload files directly to you, useful for collecting assets or submissions.
features.title: File Requests

features.description: Save and organize images and inspiration into shareable visual boards through WeTransfer's Collect tool.
features.title: Collect Moodboards

features.description: Native iOS and Android apps plus a Mac menu-bar app let you send files without opening a browser.
features.title: Mobile and Mac Apps

features.description: Paid plans include cloud storage for keeping and sharing files beyond a single one-off transfer.
features.title: Cloud Storage

features.description: Files are encrypted in transit and at rest while stored on WeTransfer's servers before download.
features.title: Encrypted Transfers

pros: Very simple, no-signup interface that lets recipients download without creating an account
Free tier covers occasional large-file sending without hitting typical email attachment limits
Distinctive, artist-designed visual experience that stands out from generic file-sharing tools
Paid plans scale reasonably from solo freelancers up to small collaborating teams
Native iOS, Android, and Mac apps make sending files convenient outside the browser
cons: Free plan's transfer and volume limits are easy for regular users to exceed
Files and links expire by design, so it isn't suited to long-term storage or archiving
Public developer API has been retired, limiting automation and custom integrations
Fewer collaboration and permission controls than full cloud-storage suites like Google Drive or Dropbox
Major layoffs following the 2024 Bending Spoons acquisition raise questions about long-term support pace

faqs.answer: Yes. The free plan allows a limited number of transfers and total data within a rolling 30-day period, with no account required for recipients to download.
faqs.question: Is WeTransfer free to use?

faqs.answer: On the free plan, links typically stay active for a few days; paid plans allow custom expiration windows that can be extended further.
faqs.question: How long do WeTransfer download links stay active?

faqs.answer: WeTransfer encrypts files in transit and at rest, and paid features like password protection and access controls add further security to transfers.
faqs.question: Is WeTransfer secure?

faqs.answer: No. Recipients simply click the link they receive by email or that you share directly, then download the files without signing up.
faqs.question: Do recipients need a WeTransfer account to download files?

faqs.answer: Following its 2024 acquisition by Bending Spoons, WeTransfer restructured its paid plans into Starter, Ultimate, and Teams tiers, replacing the older Pro and Premium naming.
faqs.question: What happened to WeTransfer's old Pro and Premium plans?

features: Up to 10 transfers or 3GB total every 30 days
Links stay active for a few days
Up to 10 transfers or 300GB total every 30 days
Unlimited transfers up to 1TB per transfer

wordpress.name: WordPress
wordpress.short_description: Free, open-source, self-hosted CMS powering millions of websites, blogs, and online stores worldwide.
wordpress.starting_price: Free (software); hosting, domains, themes, and plugins are separate costs

features.description: A drag-and-drop, block-based editor for building posts and pages without writing code.
features.title: Block Editor (Gutenberg)

features.description: Customize headers, footers, templates, and global styles using block themes.
features.title: Full Site Editing

features.description: Tens of thousands of free and premium plugins add SEO, e-commerce, forms, security, and more.
features.title: Plugin Ecosystem

features.description: Thousands of free and paid themes control the design and layout of a site.
features.title: Theme System

features.description: Built-in roles (administrator, editor, author, contributor, subscriber) control access.
features.title: User Roles and Permissions

features.description: A native REST API enables headless WordPress setups and integration with external apps.
features.title: REST API

features.description: Run and manage multiple related sites from a single WordPress installation.
features.title: Multisite Networks

features.description: Centralized management of images, video, and other uploaded files.
features.title: Media Library

features.description: Model structured content beyond blog posts, such as portfolios, products, or listings.
features.title: Custom Post Types and Taxonomies

features.description: Core and plugin-based tools support translating and localizing site content and the admin interface.
features.title: Multilingual Support

pros: Core software is free and open source with no licensing cost
Enormous plugin and theme ecosystem covers nearly any feature need
Large community, documentation, and developer talent pool make support easy to find
Highly flexible, from simple blogs to complex e-commerce and membership sites
Full control over hosting, data, and customization since it is self-hosted
cons: Requires choosing and managing your own hosting, unlike fully hosted builders
Security and performance depend heavily on keeping core, themes, and plugins updated
Quality varies widely depending on chosen theme, plugins, and hosting provider
Can become slow or bloated if too many plugins are installed
Steeper learning curve than simplified website builders for complete beginners

faqs.answer: Yes, the core WordPress software from WordPress.org is free and open source under the GPL. You still pay separately for hosting, a domain name, and any premium themes or plugins you choose to use.
faqs.question: Is WordPress really free?

faqs.answer: WordPress.org distributes the free, self-hosted software that you install on your own hosting, giving full control. WordPress.com is a separate, hosted service built on that same software but with a managed hosting plan and different pricing tiers.
faqs.question: What is the difference between WordPress.org and WordPress.com?

faqs.answer: No. Many people build and manage WordPress sites using the block editor, themes, and plugins without writing code. Coding knowledge (PHP, HTML, CSS, JavaScript) becomes useful for deeper customization.
faqs.question: Do I need coding skills to use WordPress?

faqs.answer: Yes. With plugins like WooCommerce, WordPress can power full online stores, including product catalogs, payments, shipping, and inventory management.
faqs.question: Can WordPress be used for e-commerce?

faqs.answer: WordPress core is actively maintained and patched, but overall site security depends on keeping core, themes, and plugins updated, using trusted extensions, and following good hosting and password practices.
faqs.question: Is WordPress secure?

features: Block editor and full site editing
Access to official theme and plugin directories
Self-hosted, full control over code and data

jimdo.name: Jimdo
jimdo.short_description: A German website and online store builder offering both a simple no-code editor and a code-customizable Creator editor.

Built-in tools for bookings, forms, and legal pages beyond basic site design
Free plan available, with the option to upgrade at any time
cons: No customer service phone hotline — support is via help center, live chat, or email only
Paying month-to-month costs more than committing to a 12- or 24-month term
Choosing between the Website Builder and Creator editors upfront matters, since their feature sets differ

faqs.answer: Yes, you can start with a free website and upgrade to a paid plan at any time from the site dashboard.

faqs.answer: You can pay monthly, or save money by paying upfront for a 12- or 24-month term.

faqs.answer: Consumers get a 14-day right of withdrawal where a statutory right applies; this does not apply to business accounts.
faqs.question: Can I cancel my contract?

faqs.answer: Yes, MinIO AIStor is designed to be self-hosted across edge, on-premises, and cloud environments.

faqs.question: Who develops Immich?

faqs.answer: Yes, an admin user can add and manage additional user accounts on the same Immich instance.
faqs.question: Can multiple people use one Immich server?

excalidraw.name: Excalidraw
excalidraw.short_description: Excalidraw is a free, open-source virtual whiteboard for hand-drawn style diagrams and wireframes with real-time collaboration.

features.description: An infinite canvas with a distinctive hand-drawn visual style for diagrams and sketches.
features.title: Hand-drawn style canvas

features.description: End-to-end encrypted collaborative editing via shareable links.
features.title: Real-time collaboration

features.description: Export to PNG, SVG, clipboard, or the native .excalidraw JSON format.
features.title: Flexible export

features.description: Installable as a Progressive Web App with local-first autosave.
features.title: Offline PWA support

features.description: Includes text-to-diagram and wireframe-to-code AI features.
features.title: Generative AI tools

features.description: Supports importable shape libraries and a dark mode interface.
features.title: Shape libraries & dark mode

pros: MIT-licensed and fully open source
No account required to start drawing
End-to-end encrypted real-time collaboration
Used by companies including Google Cloud, Meta, Notion, and Replit, per Excalidraw's own site
cons: Advanced team, presentation, and cloud-workspace features require the paid Excalidraw+ tier
The free version relies on browser local storage, which can be cleared unexpectedly if drawings aren't exported

faqs.answer: Yes, the core Excalidraw editor is free and open source under the MIT license.
faqs.question: Is Excalidraw free?

faqs.answer: A paid tier adding extended team collaboration, live presentations, cloud workspace management, and upgraded generative AI, with a 14-day free trial.
faqs.question: What is Excalidraw+?

faqs.answer: $7/month billed monthly, or $6/month billed annually, according to its pricing page.
faqs.question: How much does Excalidraw+ cost?

faqs.answer: No, the free web app can be used without signing up.
faqs.question: Does Excalidraw require an account?

diagrams-net.name: Diagrams.net
diagrams-net.short_description: diagrams.net (draw.io) is a free, open-source diagramming tool for flowcharts, org charts, and UML, with no sign-up required.

features.description: Collaborative editing with shared cursors.
features.title: Real-time collaboration

features.description: Diagrams can be stored in Google Drive, OneDrive, SharePoint, or locally; draw.io states it cannot access that data.
features.title: Bring-your-own storage

features.description: Native integrations with Confluence, Jira, GitHub, VS Code, Microsoft Teams, and Notion.
features.title: Productivity tool integrations

features.description: Available as a desktop app in addition to the web app.
features.title: Desktop application

features.description: Diagrams created since 2005 continue to open in the current app.
features.title: Long-term backward compatibility

features.description: An official Docker image (jgraph/drawio) lets teams run draw.io entirely on their own infrastructure.
features.title: Self-hostable

pros: Completely free with no sign-up required
Open source under the Apache 2.0 license
Deep integrations with Atlassian, Microsoft, and Google productivity tools
Can be fully self-hosted via an official Docker image
Users control where diagram data is stored
cons: The Confluence/Jira marketplace app is a separately licensed Atlassian add-on rather than part of the free base app
Interface polish can feel less refined than dedicated paid diagramming tools for advanced enterprise workflows

faqs.answer: Yes. Its homepage states it is 'Free forever' and open source, with no sign-up required.
faqs.question: Is diagrams.net free?

faqs.answer: Apache 2.0.
faqs.question: What license is draw.io released under?

faqs.answer: Users choose their own storage — Google Drive, OneDrive, SharePoint, or a local device — and draw.io states it cannot access that data.
faqs.question: Where is my diagram data stored?

faqs.answer: Yes, alongside Google Drive, Microsoft 365, GitHub, VS Code, and Notion.
faqs.question: Does draw.io integrate with Confluence and Jira?

faqs.answer: Yes, an official Docker image (jgraph/drawio) is available for running it on your own infrastructure.
faqs.question: Can draw.io be self-hosted?

cube.name: Cube
cube.short_description: Cube is an open source semantic layer and agentic analytics platform for governed BI and embedded, AI-powered analytics.

features.description: Natural-language querying grounded in a governed semantic model.
features.title: Analytics Chat

features.description: Governed analytics surfaces for internal business intelligence.
features.title: Workbooks and dashboards

features.description: Multi-tenant, AI-powered analytics embedded into customer-facing products.
features.title: Embedded analytics

features.description: Customizable programmatic access to the semantic data layer.
features.title: Core Data APIs

features.description: Works with Claude, ChatGPT, Slack, and Power BI via a DAX API.
features.title: Ecosystem integrations

features.description: A community-driven core with 18,000+ GitHub stars and 350+ contributors.
features.title: Open source semantic layer

pros: Free-forever tier available for hobbyist and small projects
Large, active open source community (18,000+ GitHub stars, 350+ contributors)
Serves both internal BI and embedded, customer-facing analytics from one semantic layer
Used in production by large companies such as Walmart, Tesla, and Webflow
cons: Paid Starter and Premium tiers are priced per developer, which can scale with team size
Enterprise-grade SLAs, BYOC, and SSO are reserved for the top paid tier
Requires modeling effort to build out the semantic layer before value is realized

faqs.answer: Yes, Cube offers a free-forever tier for small projects, plus paid Starter, Premium, and Enterprise tiers.
faqs.question: Is Cube free?

faqs.answer: The Starter plan is $40 per developer/month and Premium is $80 per developer/month; Enterprise is custom-priced.
faqs.question: How much does Cube cost?

faqs.answer: Yes, Cube maintains an open source repository with 18,000+ GitHub stars and 350+ contributors.
faqs.question: Is Cube open source?

faqs.answer: Yes, the Premium and Enterprise tiers support multi-tenant embedded dashboards and analytics chat for customers.
faqs.question: Can Cube power embedded analytics for a SaaS product?

sqlmesh.name: SQLMesh
sqlmesh.short_description: SQLMesh is an open source SQL/Python data transformation framework from Tobiko Data, with dbt-compatible pipelines and virtual environments.

features.description: Isolated development environments without extra data warehouse costs.
features.title: Virtual Data Environments

features.description: A Terraform-style workflow to preview the impact of changes before deploying.
features.title: Plan/Apply workflow

features.description: Automatic unit test generation and built-in data audits.
features.title: Automated testing and audits

features.description: Lineage tracking and pre-execution error detection.
features.title: Column-level lineage

features.description: Transpiles across 10+ SQL dialects using the SQLGlot library.
features.title: Multi-dialect SQL support

features.description: Designed to be backward compatible with existing dbt projects.
features.title: dbt compatibility

pros: Core framework is free and open source under the Apache License 2.0
Virtual environments reduce warehouse costs during development
Built-in testing, auditing, and lineage reduce the need for extra tooling
dbt-compatible, easing migration for existing dbt users
cons: Tobiko Cloud pricing isn't public and requires contacting sales
Newer project than dbt, so its community and ecosystem are smaller
Tobiko Data was acquired by Fivetran in September 2025, which may affect its roadmap

faqs.answer: Yes, the core SQLMesh framework is open source and free under the Apache License 2.0.
faqs.question: Is SQLMesh free?

faqs.answer: It's the paid commercial platform built on SQLMesh, adding collaboration, observability, and cost tracking, priced via a platform fee plus consumption.
faqs.question: What is Tobiko Cloud?

faqs.answer: Yes, SQLMesh is designed to be backward compatible with existing dbt projects.
faqs.question: Is SQLMesh compatible with dbt?

faqs.answer: SQLMesh was created by Tobiko Data, which was acquired by Fivetran in September 2025.
faqs.question: Who built SQLMesh?
